idea-plugin.name: Aurora Language Support
idea-plugin.description:
Aurora Language Support for IntelliJ IDEA

Provides first-class IDE support for the Aurora programming language — a modern, statically typed language with Kotlin/Rust-inspired syntax that compiles to efficient JVM bytecode.

Features

  • File Type Recognition — Automatically recognizes .ar source files and associates them with the Aurora language.
  • Syntax Highlighting — Full token-level highlighting for keywords, type keywords, modifiers, identifiers, string literals, numeric literals, boolean/null literals, operators, and comments.
  • Error & Warning Annotations — Real-time diagnostics powered by the Aurora compiler. Parse errors and type-check violations are underlined directly in the editor as you type.
  • Documentation Popups (Quick Doc) — Press Ctrl+Q / F1 on any symbol to view its signature with syntax-highlighted code, doc-comment content, and parameter details in a rich HTML popup.

Aurora Language Highlights

  • Static typing with full null safety (? nullable types, ?. safe calls, ?: Elvis operator)
  • Modern control flow: if expressions, match pattern matching, for/while/repeat-until loops
  • Object-oriented programming: classes, traits, abstract classes, inheritance, override
  • Functional style: lambdas, higher-order functions, extension functions
  • Flexible error handling: Result<T, E> type and try-catch-finally
  • Concurrency: async/await coroutines and OS-level thread expressions
  • Rich standard library: Io, Collections, Concurrent modules

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.3 (build 243)
